sectionOrderRules: Hero must state the business type and the main offered materials or extraction focus, with a primary contact CTA.
Material-summary must appear before the final contact CTA so visitors quickly understand what is offered.
Operations-summary should explain capacity, delivery or handling in concrete terms without making unverifiable claims.
Trust-proof must use real business facts such as organisation details, references, visible location information or practical experience when provided.
Avoid repeating the same material list in multiple sections with identical wording.
Materials intro must connect the offered materials to likely buyer needs in plain language.
Material-list items must be specific, for example material type, fraction, intended use or delivery format when known.
Use-cases should stay factual and describe suitable applications without promising performance that is not supported by supplied data.
If pricing-note is shown it must frame pricing as request-based or variable unless exact prices are actually supplied.
Operations intro must explain what the site does in practical business terms, not generic marketing copy.
Process-overview should describe extraction, sorting, processing or handling only at a level supported by the dossier.
Logistics-and-delivery must clarify pickup, transport, delivery area or coordination details when known.
Responsibility-and-safety should present factual routines, site rules or environmental consideration without inventing certificates, permits or compliance claims.
Contact info must include real phone, email and address details when available and must not contain placeholders.
Contact form should be positioned after core contact details so urgent visitors can call directly.
Location and opening-hours should help visitors plan visits, pickups or deliveries.
If map is shown it should support the address and access information rather than replace written directions.
